header.title: SyncTime
header.subtitle: Check the target server's current time before ticketing or limited edition purchases to minimize time discrepancies with your local clock.

form.label: Server URL to Compare
form.hint: Enter the URL of the site you want to check (protocol optional)

clock.performanceNote: * May slightly impact performance when enabled.

notification.title: Notification Settings
notification.subtitle: Get notified when target time is reached.

content.ntpDescription: This service shows an approximate current time by referencing the HTTP Date header from the target server. For more precise timing, we recommend synchronizing your device with an NTP server in advance.

features.title: Precision Timing
features.description: In ticketing and limited edition purchases, 0.1 seconds can determine success or failure. Server time synchronization is essential.

features.title: HTTP Date Analysis
features.description: We analyze the HTTP Date header from target servers to estimate their current time with millisecond precision.

features.title: Smart Alerts
features.description: Set target time alarms to be notified exactly when you need to take action, ensuring you never miss critical moments.

whatIsServerTime.title: What is Server Time?
whatIsServerTime.content: Server time refers to the system clock maintained by the web server hosting a website. Unlike your local computer or smartphone time, which can drift or be manually adjusted, server time is typically synchronized with authoritative time sources. When participating in time-sensitive events like concert ticket sales or course registration, the difference between your local time and the server time can mean the difference between success and disappointment. Even a 2-second discrepancy can result in missing out on limited availability items.

whyImportant.title: Why is Server Time Synchronization Critical?
whyImportant.content: Modern e-commerce and ticketing systems process thousands of requests per second during high-demand events. These systems use server-side timestamps to determine the order of incoming requests. If your local clock is ahead of the server by even 1 second, you might think you're clicking at the exact release time, but the server sees your request as arriving early and may reject it. Conversely, if your clock is behind, you're already competing with users who submitted earlier. SyncTime helps you identify and compensate for these time differences.

howItWorks.title: How SyncTime Measures Server Time
steps: You enter the target website URL in the input field above
SyncTime sends an HTTP HEAD request to that server
The server responds with a Date header containing its current time
We calculate network latency (Round-Trip Time) to improve accuracy
The estimated server time is displayed with millisecond precision
You can now see the exact time difference between your device and the server

items.title: Check Server Time 5 Minutes Early
items.description: Use SyncTime to check the target server's time at least 5 minutes before the ticket release. Note the time difference.

items.title: Remember the Time Offset
items.description: If your computer is 1.5 seconds ahead of the server, mentally adjust your clicking time by that amount.

items.title: Practice Refresh Timing
items.description: Optimal refresh timing is typically 1-2 seconds before the exact release time (server time basis).

items.title: Use Wired Internet
items.description: Wired connections provide more stable latency compared to Wi-Fi, improving time synchronization accuracy.

items.title: Close Unnecessary Tabs
items.description: Browser performance impacts your ability to act quickly. Close all unnecessary tabs and extensions.

items.title: Consider NTP Synchronization
items.description: For maximum accuracy, synchronize your device with an NTP server before the event. This ensures your local clock is as accurate as possible.

practiceGame.title: Ticketing Practice Game
practiceGame.subtitle: Train your click reaction speed
practiceGame.instructions: Click the appearing targets as fast as you can. How many can you click in 30 seconds?

ticketmaster.intro: Ticketmaster is the world's leading ticketing platform for concerts, sports, theater, and live entertainment. With millions of users competing for popular events, precise server time synchronization is crucial for securing tickets.

tips.title: Join the Waiting Room Early
tips.description: Ticketmaster uses a virtual waiting room system. Join at least 10 minutes before the on-sale time to get a better queue position.

tips.title: Have Payment Ready
tips.description: Save your payment information in advance. You typically have only 5-10 minutes to complete checkout once you select seats.

tips.title: Use the Mobile App
tips.description: The Ticketmaster app often provides a smoother experience than the website during high-traffic events.

warnings: Ticketmaster may implement CAPTCHA verification during high-demand sales.
Multiple browser tabs or devices may result in being kicked out of the queue.
Prices may include dynamic pricing that increases based on demand.

stubhub.intro: StubHub is a leading ticket resale marketplace where fans buy and sell tickets to sports, concerts, and theater events. Timing is important for snagging good deals before they're gone.

tips.title: Set Price Alerts
tips.description: Use StubHub's price alert feature to be notified when tickets drop to your target price.

tips.title: Check Last-Minute Deals
tips.description: Prices often drop significantly 24-48 hours before an event as sellers try to offload tickets.

tips.title: FanProtect Guarantee
tips.description: StubHub's FanProtect guarantee ensures you get valid tickets or your money back.

warnings: Ticket prices on StubHub can be higher than face value due to resale markups.
Delivery methods vary - check if tickets are mobile, print-at-home, or physical.

amazon.intro: Amazon is the world's largest online retailer. Lightning Deals and limited-time offers require split-second timing to secure popular items before they sell out.

tips.title: Enable 1-Click Ordering
tips.description: Set up 1-Click ordering with your preferred payment and shipping address to checkout instantly.

tips.title: Watch Lightning Deals
tips.description: Lightning Deals have a countdown timer. Add items to your cart before the deal goes live.

tips.title: Use Amazon Prime
tips.description: Prime members often get early access to deals and faster checkout options.

warnings: Lightning Deals have limited quantities and may sell out within seconds.
Adding to cart doesn't reserve the item - you must complete checkout.
Some deals are Prime-exclusive and require a membership.

ebay.intro: eBay is a global online marketplace for auctions and fixed-price sales. Auction sniping - bidding at the last second - requires precise timing to win competitive items.

tips.title: Snipe Auctions Strategically
tips.description: Place your maximum bid in the final 5-10 seconds to avoid driving up the price early.

tips.title: Set Maximum Bid
tips.description: Decide your maximum price beforehand and stick to it to avoid overpaying in the heat of bidding.

tips.title: Check Seller Ratings
tips.description: Always verify seller feedback and ratings before bidding on high-value items.

warnings: eBay's auto-bidding system may outbid you if another user has a higher maximum.
International shipping can add significant costs and delays.
Auction end times are in the seller's local timezone - verify before sniping.

eventbrite.intro: Eventbrite is a popular platform for discovering and booking tickets to local events, conferences, and festivals. Popular free events can fill up quickly, making timing essential.

tips.title: Follow Event Organizers
tips.description: Follow organizers to get notified when new events are posted or tickets become available.

tips.title: Register Early for Free Events
tips.description: Free events often have limited capacity and can sell out within minutes of being posted.

tips.title: Check Waitlists
tips.description: If an event is sold out, join the waitlist - tickets often become available as people cancel.

warnings: Some events have tiered pricing that increases as the event date approaches.
Free events may still have service fees at checkout.
